title: Can't start a podman machine as any other use than the one who installed podman (macOS)po
body:
### Issue Description

No other use seems to be able to run podman instances, which makes 'install for all users' incorrect.


```
podman version
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49619: connect: connection refused




```

As the original user:

```
podman version
Client:        Podman Engine
Version:       5.7.1
API Version:   5.7.1
Go Version:    go1.25.5
Git Commit:    f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:         Wed Dec 10 06:58:11 2025
Build Origin:  pkginstaller
OS/Arch:       darwin/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.7.1
API Version:  5.7.1
Go Version:   go1.25.4 X:nodwarf5
Git Commit:   f845d14e941889ba4c071f35233d09b29d363c75
Built:        Mon Dec  8 16:00:00 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64


```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.Install podman for all users on MacOS
1. create a new account.
1. podman machine init somename
1.podman machine start somename

observe hang forever

### Describe the results you received

connection reset by peer or connection refused depending on the operation

### Describe the results you expected

For it to work

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: By scrubbing podman off the machine and reinstalling it in from the second, non-admin account, it can successfully start machines. In both accounts. Even after a restart. I don't know what this means.
please tell us how you installed podman and from where
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Okay, I figured out what the real problem is here. I'll open a separate issue for it.

Trying to set up a launchctl for MacOS. I thought I had it working right, but it's failing on startup and not giving me a decent diagnostic output.

Something about it trying to start over and over again in a loop is giving the CLI some indications that podman is running even though it isn't, so partway through trying to do things it errors out.
